chunk_summary: This section focuses on age-structured IYCF counseling and minimum viable changes to improve pediatric nutrition, emphasizing breastfeeding, complementary feeding, and linkage to Anganwadi services under POSHAN 2.0 guidelines.
learning_objectives: Understand the age-structured approach to IYCF counseling for 0–6 months and 6–23 months.
Learn to identify and implement minimum viable changes in feeding and hygiene practices.
Recognize the importance of linking families to Anganwadi services and supplementary nutrition programs.
Align clinic counseling with POSHAN 2.0 behavior-change messaging for improved adherence.
key_facts: For 0–6 months, assess exclusive breastfeeding, latch, frequency, and milk supply before considering formula.
For 6–23 months, evaluate meal frequency, dietary diversity, texture progression, and responsive feeding.
POSHAN 2.0 emphasizes behavior change in breastfeeding and complementary feeding practices.
Minimum viable changes include identifying one protein source, one iron-rich food with vitamin C, and one hygiene behavior.
Counseling without scheduled follow-up is rarely effective in real-world settings.
reference_quote: POSHAN 2.0 behavior-change content explicitly targets breastfeeding and complementary feeding, and aligning clinic messages with frontline worker messaging improves adherence.
document_key_topics: Minimum viable changes in nutrition and hygiene
Linkage to Anganwadi and POSHAN 2.0 programs
